extraction.one_line_summary: LUTSENKO Alex survived two crashes in final 20km, attacked early on closing climb, held off FUGLSANG and YATES by seconds
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ attacked on Capo Giugno climb when teammate FUGLSANG sat in chase, maximizing Jumbo numerical advantage despite mechanical chaos.
LUTSENKO counterattacked when break splintered on early climb slopes, gambling nobody would organize chase with GC riders watching each other.
YATES sat on wheels refusing to chase DUMOULIN despite Sunweb threat, prioritizing seconds over teammate CLARKE's stage chances.
Crashes at 20km and 2km decimated field, suggesting finale's technical descent/corners matter more than pure climbing for GC positioning.

rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: won from early move despite two crashes, signals exceptional resilience and opportunism on hard finales
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: second place after team numerics forced rivals' hands, confirms form building toward Classics
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: third but lost 2 seconds to ROGLIČ through tactical passivity despite stronger team position
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: gained 2 seconds on YATES through aggressive climbing despite mechanical chaos, strongest GC card played
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: lost 23 seconds in late crash, effectively ending GC, suggests cornering vulnerability remains chronic issue
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: lost 23 seconds in same crash as DUMOULIN, rare positioning error in finale chaos
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: neutral: finished alone 9 seconds down, isolated after break split but held decent GC position
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: abandoned after crash, major loss for Bahrain's Classics campaign
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: in 10-man break suggesting either recovery ride or GC already conceded after poor start
